task_completion.description: Verify that the Kruskal-Wallis nonparametric test is set up correctly in JASP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the JASP analysis panel (ANOVA > ANOVA or Nonparametric), 'len' should be in the Dependent Variable box and 'dose' should be in the Fixed Factors/Grouping Variable box.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Kruskal-Wallis test, Dunn's post-hoc tests, and descriptive statistics are generated in JASP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JASP results panel on the right should display tables for 'Kruskal-Wallis Test', 'Descriptives', and 'Post Hoc Comparisons' (specifically Dunn's test).

task_completion.description: Verify that the JASP analysis file is saved to the correct location.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JASP window title should show 'ToothGrowth_KruskalWallis.jasp', or a file manager/terminal window should show the file saved at '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/ToothGrowth_KruskalWallis.jasp'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the text report file is created with the exact required labels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the file 'kruskal_wallis_report.txt' containing the exact labels: 'Kruskal-Wallis H:', 'Degrees of Freedom:', 'p-value:', 'Pairwise Comparison 0.5 vs 1:', 'Pairwise Comparison 0.5 vs 2:', 'Pairwise Comparison 1 vs 2:', and 'Conclusion:'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the main Kruskal-Wallis statistics in the report are correct.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text report should show an H-statistic of approximately 40.67 (allow 39.67 to 41.67), Degrees of Freedom as 2, and a p-value indicating significance (e.g., < 0.001).

task_completion.description: Verify that the post-hoc results and conclusion in the report are correct.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text report should state 'significant' for all three pairwise comparisons (0.5 vs 1, 0.5 vs 2, 1 vs 2) and include a one-sentence conclusion summarizing the findings.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the JASP graphical interface to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the JASP application open with the ToothGrowth dataset and analysis panels active, rather than just running a Python or R script in the terminal.

integrity.description: Verify that the results typed into the text report match the actual output shown in JASP.
integrity.visual_evidence: The statistical values in the text editor should match the numbers visible in the JASP results panel tables.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent did not just use a terminal command to echo hardcoded text into the report file without doing the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be evidence of the agent extracting the values from JASP (e.g., viewing the JASP results) before or while writing to the text file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct Kruskal-Wallis test results for 'len' grouped by 'dose' are: H-statistic = 40.67 (acceptable range 39.67 - 41.67), df = 2, and p-value < 0.001. Dunn's post-hoc tests will show that all three pairwise comparisons (0.5 vs 1, 0.5 vs 2, and 1 vs 2) are statistically significant. The expected output files are /home/ga/Documents/JASP/ToothGrowth_KruskalWallis.jasp and /home/ga/Documents/JASP/kruskal_wallis_report.txt.
